parameters.systemDirective:
You are a proposal generator for Axiom Nexus. Return one STRICT JSON object only. The parser will normalize and validate final execution.

You are the tool-planning brain for Axiom Nexus.
Return STRICT JSON only. No prose, no markdown, no backticks.

Output schema exactly:
{
  "action": "write_file" | "read_file" | "list_directory",
  "path": "absolute path or filename",
  "writeMode": "overwrite" | "append" | "line_edit",
  "content": "final text to write when not line_edit",
  "lineEdits": [
    { "line": 2, "text": "replacement text" }
  ],
  "append": true | false,
  "confidence": 0.00-1.00
}

Rules:
- Resolve references like "it" / "that file" from context.
- If user gives line-based instructions, use writeMode="line_edit" + lineEdits.
- For factual placeholders, return final factual value (example: "capital of Slovakia" => "Bratislava").
- If user asks for N items and spacing (example: "each two lines"), generate exactly N entries with correct blank lines in content.
- Keep paths inside C:/Users/Martin/.n8n-files/Axiom_Files unless explicitly told otherwise.
- Confidence must reflect ambiguity.
- If the user says "it"/"that file"/"latest file" without a new path, you must reuse the provided target file path exactly.

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ $json.allowEmptyWrite ? ("I have successfully created an empty file at " + ($json.fullPath || $json.fileName || $json.path || "C:/Users/Martin/.n8n-files/Axiom_Files") + ".") : ("I have successfully written the file to " + ($json.fullPath || $json.fileName || $json.path || "C:/Users/Martin/.n8n-files/Axiom_Files") + ".") }}

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ (() => { const dirPath = String($json.fullPath || $json.path || "C:/Users/Martin/.n8n-files/Axiom_Files").replace(/\\/g, "/"); const names = $input.all().map(i => String(i.json.fileName || "").trim()).filter(Boolean); if (!names.length) return `Directory ${dirPath} is empty.`; return `Directory contents (${dirPath}):\n` + names.map((n, i) => `${i + 1}. ${n}`).join("\n"); })() }}

parameters.systemDirective: You are OpenClaw, processing an automation task. The user is outlining a multi-step objective for you.

You are Axiom Agent for Axiom Hub.

Rules:
- Answer directly and concisely.
- If LIVE WEB CONTEXT is present, treat it as retrieved internet data and use it in your answer.
- When LIVE WEB CONTEXT is present, never claim that you cannot browse the internet.
- If the user explicitly asks for live/current web data and LIVE WEB CONTEXT is missing, ask them to enable Internet search (globe button) and resend.
- Do not mention model cutoffs, browsing limits, or internal capabilities unless the user explicitly asks.
- Do not add roleplay commentary.
- For factual questions: provide the best answer first, then a short uncertainty note only if needed.
- Keep tone neutral and professional.
parameters.userInput:
={{ "INTERNET SEARCH: " + ($node["Extract Input"].json.internetSearchEnabled ? "enabled" : "disabled") + "

" + ($node["Extract Input"].json.historyText ? ("RECENT CONTEXT:
" + $node["Extract Input"].json.historyText + "

") : "") + ($json.webContext ? ("LIVE WEB CONTEXT (latest snippets, verify if critical):
" + $json.webContext + "

") : "") + $node["Extract Input"].json.content }}

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ (($json.response && String($json.response).trim().length > 0) ? $json.response : ($json.message || "Workflow completed, but no response payload was generated.")) }}

string.name: contextLastFilePath
string.value: ={{ (() => { const norm = (p) => String(p || "").replace(/\\/g, "/").trim(); const looksLikeFile = (p) => /[\/][^\/]+\.[A-Za-z0-9]{1,10}$/.test(String(p || "")); const fromJson = norm($json.contextLastFilePath || ""); const fromExtract = norm($node["Extract Input"].json.lastFilePath || ""); const prev = looksLikeFile(fromJson) ? fromJson : (looksLikeFile(fromExtract) ? fromExtract : ""); const candidate = norm($json.destinationPath || $json.fullPath || $json.path || ""); const action = String($json.action || "").toLowerCase(); const status = String($json.status || "").toLowerCase(); if (status === "success") { if (action === "delete_file") return ""; if (action === "list_directory") return prev; if (looksLikeFile(candidate)) return candidate; } return prev; })() }}

parameters.name: message
parameters.value: ={{ "Axiom Nexus is requesting " + ($json.action || "operation") + " access outside sandbox. Source: " + ($json.fullPath || "(none)") + ($json.destinationPath ? (" | Destination: " + $json.destinationPath) : "") + ". Allow?" }}

parameters.systemDirective:
You are a proposal generator for Axiom Nexus. Return one STRICT JSON object only. The parser will normalize and validate final execution.

You are the high-accuracy planning brain for Axiom Nexus.
Return one STRICT JSON object only.

Schema:
{
  "action": "write_file" | "read_file" | "list_directory",
  "path": "absolute path or filename",
  "writeMode": "overwrite" | "append" | "line_edit",
  "content": "final write text",
  "lineEdits": [
    { "line": 2, "text": "replacement text" }
  ],
  "append": true | false,
  "confidence": 0.00-1.00
}

Hard requirements:
- No markdown, no explanations.
- Resolve "it/that file" and use provided target path.
- For line instructions, always return explicit numeric lineEdits.
- Resolve factual placeholders into concrete values (example: Slovakia capital -> Bratislava).
- Respect cardinality and spacing requests (example: "3 biggest capitals", "each two lines").
- Prefer coherent final content over meta-instructions.
- Keep path within C:/Users/Martin/.n8n-files/Axiom_Files unless user explicitly requests otherwise.
- If the user says "it"/"that file"/"latest file" without a new path, reuse the provided target file path exactly.

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ $json.verification ? ("Execution verification failed for " + ($json.verification.checkedPath || $json.fullPath || "target") + ": " + ($json.verification.reason || "Unknown reason") + ".") : ("Execution failed: " + ($json.message || $json.error || "Unknown execution error") + ".") }}

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ $json.chatGuardMessage || "This looks like a file/tool operation. Switch to Direct Command to run it safely." }}

string.name: response
string.value: =I have successfully deleted the file at {{$json.path || $json.fullPath || "target file"}}.

string.name: response
string.value: ={{ (() => { const norm = (p) => String(p || "").replace(/\\\\/g, "/"); const src = norm($json.sourcePath || ""); const dst = norm($json.destinationPath || $json.fullPath || ""); const srcName = src.split("/").pop() || "source"; const dstName = dst.split("/").pop() || "destination"; const srcDir = src.split("/").slice(0, -1).join("/").toLowerCase(); const dstDir = dst.split("/").slice(0, -1).join("/").toLowerCase(); if (srcDir && dstDir && srcDir === dstDir) { return `I have successfully renamed ${srcName} to ${dstName}.`; } return `I have successfully moved the file from ${src || "source file"} to ${dst || "destination file"}.`; })() }}
